checks.name: GAP_ASSESSMENT.md classifies every LENS traceback artifact
checks.detail: 7 layers classified: Layer 0 (source) through Layer 7 (executive projection). 4 infrastructure/binding categories. All artifacts have GENERAL/PARAMETERIZABLE/BLUEEDGE_SPECIFIC classification.

checks.name: GAP_ASSESSMENT.md quantifies generalization ratio
checks.detail: 52 .js files analyzed. ~88% runtime engine general, ~6% parameterizable, ~6% BlueEdge-specific. Summary table with by-file-count and by-functional-role breakdowns.

checks.name: GAP_ASSESSMENT.md documents the impact chain for Client C
checks.detail: Full cascade: semantic_topology_model ABSENT → crosswalk EMPTY → reconciliation NULL → grounding_ratio undefined → Q-class undefined → LENS semantic zones EMPTY → SQO stays at S1. What works and what doesn't documented.

checks.name: GAP_ASSESSMENT.md includes BlueEdge-Specific Assumptions That MUST NOT Leak subsection
checks.detail: Section 5: 9 assumptions documented (17 DOMAINs, 42 capabilities, 89 components, DOM-09, grounding ratio, Q-02, DOMAIN names, DOMAIN types, confidence thresholds).

checks.name: CSR spec defines JSON schema
checks.detail: Full JSON Schema 2020-12 with required fields: schema_version, client_id, domains[], capabilities[], components[], metadata{}. Pattern validation for IDs.

checks.name: CSR spec documents CEU analogy AND disanalogy
checks.detail: Section 5: 5 analogy points (structural role, location, pipeline position, versioning, client isolation) and 6 disanalogy points (construction method, automation, time, repeatability, error mode, evolution).

checks.name: CSR spec classifies as SPECIFIED_NOT_IMPLEMENTED
checks.detail: Maturity: SPECIFIED_NOT_IMPLEMENTED stated in header and Section 6.

checks.name: CSR spec defines advisory construction protocol
checks.detail: Section 6: 6-step protocol (Evidence Review → Domain Identification → Capability Classification → Component Mapping → Review Gate → Versioning and Lock).

checks.name: CSR spec separates semantic ontology authoring (human) from topology generation (deterministic)
checks.detail: Section 1: Critical distinction table. Semantic Ontology Authoring = human/governed. Semantic Topology Generation = deterministic from CSR. Decoupled from build_semantic_layer.py.

checks.name: CSR spec includes BlueEdge-Specific Assumptions That MUST NOT Leak subsection
checks.detail: Section 7: 8 assumptions documented with 'Why It Must Not Leak' justification for each.

checks.name: Crosswalk spec defines auto-derivation interface
checks.detail: Section 2: Inputs (5 artifacts), Outputs (3 artifacts), Proposal Schema (JSON), Confidence Scoring (4 ranges).

checks.name: Crosswalk spec specifies human review gate
checks.detail: Section 3: 7-step review process with auto_accept_threshold and review_threshold. Review Gate Output schema.

checks.name: Crosswalk spec addresses irresolvability detection
checks.detail: Section 4: Detection algorithm (3 conditions), Detection Output schema, 3 handling strategies (accept, split DOM, map to dominant).

checks.name: Lifecycle spec defines all 8 phases with automated/advisory classification
checks.detail: Section 1: 8 phases (0-8) with Name, Automated?, Duration, Maturity columns.

checks.name: Lifecycle spec Phase 3 = Semantic Ontology Authoring, Phase 4 = Semantic Topology Generation (distinct)
checks.detail: Phase 3: 'Semantic Ontology Authoring (CSR)' — NO advisory. Phase 4: 'Semantic Topology Generation (from CSR)' — YES deterministic.

checks.name: Lifecycle spec integrates with existing SQO 15-stage lifecycle
checks.detail: Section 5: Mapping table from 8 onboarding phases to 15 SQO lifecycle stages.

checks.name: Lifecycle spec formalizes S0→S1 gate
checks.detail: Section 3: Gate requirements table (5 requirements), What S1 Delivers (7 items), What S1 Does NOT Deliver (7 items). CSR NOT required for S1.

checks.name: Marketplace assessment addresses 'built today' claim precision
checks.detail: Section 2: PATH A = fully built and general (TRUE). PATH B = built for BlueEdge, requires per-client advisory authoring (FALSE as universal claim). Corrected claim provided.

checks.name: Marketplace assessment contains irreducible governance statement
checks.detail: Section 1: 'Semantic qualification is not a temporary onboarding inconvenience. It is an architecturally permanent governed layer.' — prominent placement with full justification.

checks.name: Marketplace assessment specifies advisory as architecturally permanent
checks.detail: Section 5: 5 reasons why advisory is permanent. Evolution table (4 phases: current → CSR → auto-derivation → multi-client). Role evolution, not elimination.

checks.name: Hardcoding fix specs cover DOM-04, flagshipBinding, compile_blueedge
checks.detail: 3 fixes: Fix 1 (DOM-04 passthrough, LOW), Fix 2 (flagshipBinding defaults, TRIVIAL), Fix 3 (compile_blueedge_correspondence.js parameterization, MEDIUM). Each with current code, fix approach, effort.

checks.name: No terminology collision with TERMINOLOGY_LOCK.md
checks.detail: 'Client Semantic Registry' verified against 60+ locked terms before execution. No collision. Term now added to TERMINOLOGY_LOCK.md.
